shortDescription: Prepare, watch, and explicitly land GitHub pull requests.
longDescription: PR Completion packages four coordinated skills for validating and committing local work, creating pull requests, watching GitHub gates, repairing CI failures, triaging review feedback, and resolving conflicts. At verified readiness it asks separately for each PR and exact head before a guarded protected auto-merge or merge-queue request, then observes the request until merged or blocked. It never uses admin or protection bypass, force-push, history rewrite, implicit approval, or direct merge APIs.
authentication: Uses the user's existing local GitHub CLI authentication; the plugin operates no credential service and stores no credentials.
dataHandling: Runs locally with no plugin-operated backend or telemetry. Repository and pull-request data is sent only through tools the user invokes, principally GitHub via gh.
